mnemonics: PAPA Likes Making Awesome Charts: Pyplot state, Axes objects, Plot arguments, Annotations
keyPoints: Understand the difference between the pyplot (plt) stateful interface and the explicit object-oriented (fig, ax) interface.
Use the object-oriented interface (fig, ax = plt.subplots()) for complex plots to have more control.
Customize plots using arguments within plot functions (e.g., color='red', linestyle='--', marker='o') and add labels/titles with ax.set_xlabel(), ax.set_title(), etc.
commonMistakes: Forgot to call plt.show() to display the plot, especially in non-interactive environments.
Overlapping plots because plt.plot() was called multiple times without creating a new figure or clearing the current one.
Not saving figures with a high enough DPI for publications, resulting in blurry images.
